string.text:
The PS90 is a civilian market version of the Fabrique Nationale Herstal P90 submachine gun that was developed in the late 1980s as a PDW (Personal Defense Weapon) for troops whose duties do not necessitate full-sized rifles or where said rifles would be cumbersome. The primary differences from the military/law enforcement models are the semi-auto trigger group, lenghtened 16 inch barrel to comply with US gun laws, and being sold with lower capacity 30 round magazines.
 Despite the relative scarcity of 5.7mm weapons it should be possible to source 50-round magazines from somewhere within the zone, and a skilled gunsmith will be able to convert this weapon to full auto, given the right tools and parts for the job.
 %c[ui_gray_2]ATTACHMENTS: 
 %c[d_purple] - %c[ui_gray_2] Scopes: Most sights compatible with Picatinny/weaver type rails.
Find a source for quality original law enforcement/military grade magazines.
Allows for Semi-Auto and Full-Auto fire modes.
Compact 30-round helical magazine used in the PS90.
